---
title: "Publications"
---
## In peer-reviewed journals

1. **Lissón P.** & Trujillo-González, V. (accepted): Transferts interlangues chez des apprenants hispanophones de FLE en L3 : étude de cas dans un contexte universitaire plurilingue. *Synergies*. 

2. **Lissón P.** (2017). Investigating the use of readability metrics to detect differences in written productions of
learners: a corpus-based study. *Bellaterra Journal of Teaching & Learning Language & Literature , 10 (4), 68-86.* DOI: [10.5565/rev/jtl3.752](https://doi.org/10.5565/rev/jtl3.752)

## In Proceedings 
1. Ballier, N., Gaillat, T., Arnold, T.,**Lissón P.** (accepted). Predicting CEFRL levels in learner English on the basis of metrics and full texts. *Conférence sur l'Apprentissage Automatique (CAP 2018), Rouen, France*.

2. Ballier, N. & **Lissón P.** (2017). R-based strategies for DH in English Linguistics: a case study. Teaching NLP for Digital Humanities, *workshop of the German Society for Computational Linguistics conference (GSCL 2017)*.In P. Bockwinkel, T. Declerck, S. Kübler, & H. Zinsmeister (Eds.), Proceedings of the Workshop on Teaching NLP for Digital Humanities (Vol. 1918, pp. 1–10). Berlin, Germany: CEUR Workshop Proceedings. [[article]](<http://ceur-ws.org/Vol-1918/ballier.pdf>)

## Under review
1. Arnold, T., Ballier, N., **Lissón, P.**, Tilton, L. Beyond Lexical Frequencies: Using R for Text Analysis in the Digital Humanities. Submitted to *Language Resources and Evaluation*.

## In preparation (draft available)
1. Balvet, A., Ballier N. & **Lissón P.** Head nouns of noun complement clauses as discourse markers: assessing the diversity, flexibility and semantic compositionality of N+THAT structures. 

2. **Lissón P.** & Ballier, N. Investigating lexical progression through lexical diversity metrics and vocabulary growth curves in a corpus of French L3.
